steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Let $u = x^2$ to turn the quartic into a quadratic: $x^4 - x^2 - 6$ becomes $u^2 - u - 6$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Factor the quadratic $u^2 - u - 6$. Find two numbers that multiply to $-6$ and add to $-1$: those numbers are $+2$ and $-3$. So $u^2 - u - 6 = (u + 2)(u - 3)$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Verify: $(x^2 + 2)(x^2 - 3) = x^4 - 3x^2 + 2x^2 - 6 = x^4 - x^2 - 6$ ✓. That is the correct answer.
  • $(x^2+1)(x^2-6)$ expands to $x^4 - 5x^2 - 6$, so the middle term is wrong.
  • $(x^2+2)(x^2-3)$ expands to $x^4 - x^2 - 6$, matching the original expression.
  • $(x^2+3)(x^2-2)$ expands to $x^4 + x^2 - 6$, so the middle term has the wrong sign.
  • $(x^2+6)(x^2-1)$ expands to $x^4 + 5x^2 - 6$, so the middle term is wrong.
